pattern: BFS from the top-left, expanding only to neighbors that are bidirectionally connected via matching street tile openings.
hints: Each tile type exposes exactly two openings (directions); a move from cell A to adjacent cell B is only valid when A's opening faces B and B's opening faces A.
Precompute for each tile type which two directions it connects, then BFS from (0,0) using a visited grid — only enqueue a neighbor when the connection is bidirectional.
Build a lookup: tile -> set of (dr,dc) it connects, and check that the neighbor tile also connects back in the opposite direction before adding it to the queue.

constraints.note: m rows in [1,300], n cols in [1,300]

edge_inputs.description: 1x1 grid — start equals end, always true
canonical example: mismatched tiles break the path
single row where last tile faces the wrong direction
single row of left-right tiles connecting all the way
single column of up-down tiles connecting all the way
2x2 where a corner-turn path reaches the end
2x2 where tiles look close but do not connect bidirectionally
path exists but requires a winding route, not the direct diagonal

stress.description: random 20x20 grid of tiles 1-6, exercises BFS termination and visited tracking
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, size)
    n = max(1, size)
    grid = [[rng.randint(1, 6) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
